pr.title: add enum for minor scenes/class id
pr.author: sadkellz

summary: Added a MinorSceneKind enum to src/melee/gm/forward.h, enumerating minor scene/class IDs from MN_TITLE through MN_CAMERA_VS with MN_COUNT as the sentinel. The change is header-only and provides named constants plus offset comments and contextual notes for menus, cutscenes, movies, tournament screens, memory card prompts, staff roll, and other game-mode minor scenes. decomp.dev reported no GALE01 changes, so this was a semantic/naming improvement without binary impact.

key_files.reason: Added typedef enum MinorSceneKind immediately after MajorSceneKind and before forward declarations for MajorScene, MinorScene, and MinorSceneHandler.

what_changed: Introduced typedef enum MinorSceneKind in gm/forward.h.
Defined minor scene IDs with explicit ordinal comments from /* +00 */ MN_TITLE to /* +2D */ MN_COUNT.
Named common menu and gameplay-flow scenes including MN_MENU, MN_VS, MN_SUDDEN_DEATH, MN_TRAINING_MODE, MN_RESULTS, MN_DEBUG_MENU, MN_CSS, and MN_SSS.
Named toy/trophy and one-player mode scenes including MN_TOY_GALLERY, MN_TOY_LOTTERY, MN_TOY_COLLECTION, MN_INTRO_NORMAL, MN_REGEND_TOYFALL, and MN_REGEND_CONGRATS.

Named multiple cutscene entries and documented associated asset or scene strings such as Vi0102.dat/visual0102Scene, Vi0401.dat/visual0401Scene, Vi1101.dat/visual1101Scene, and Vi1202.dat/visual1202Scene.
Kept uncertain or unused entries as placeholders, including MN_0x6 and MN_0xA, with comments noting skipped/empty table behavior.

Added later flow/interface scenes such as MN_GAMEOVER, MN_COMING_SOON, MN_TOU, MN_TOU_BRACKET, MN_TOU_FINISH, MN_PRIZE_INTERFACE, MN_PROG_SCAN, MN_APPROACH, MN_MEMCARD, MN_STAFFROLL, and MN_CAMERA_VS.
smart_moves: Used an enum instead of raw numeric class IDs, making future game-mode and scene-table work easier to search and safer to read.
Preserved exact numeric ordering with offset comments, which is useful when cross-referencing scene tables or class ID arrays.
Documented uncertain entries honestly rather than overclaiming; MN_0x6 and MN_0xA retain placeholder names with notes about skipped or empty table entries.
Attached asset filenames and internal scene string names to cutscene enum entries, creating useful breadcrumbs for future data, asset, or relocation work.
Included MN_COUNT as a sentinel, matching the existing MajorSceneKind pattern nearby.
decomp_lessons: Header-only enum additions can improve readability and searchability without affecting matching; decomp.dev reported 'No changes' for GALE01.
When reconstructing ID spaces, keep the original ordinal layout visible with comments so later table decompilation can verify index alignment quickly.
For incomplete knowledge, prefer stable placeholder names such as MN_0x6/MN_0xA plus explanatory comments over speculative semantic names.
Enums for scene IDs should live in shared forward/type headers when they are likely to be referenced across multiple game-mode files.
Asset names and known scene symbol strings in comments are valuable decomp anchors, especially for minor scenes whose public-facing names may differ from internal data names.
naming_conventions: Minor scene enum type follows existing PascalCase kind naming: MinorSceneKind, analogous to MajorSceneKind.
Minor scene constants use uppercase MN_ prefix.
Unknown numeric entries use hexadecimal-style placeholder names matching the table index, e.g. MN_0x6 and MN_0xA.
Sentinel uses MN_COUNT, mirroring MJ_COUNT in MajorSceneKind.
Scene categories are reflected in names: MN_CUTSCENE_*, MN_MOVIE_*, MN_INTRO_*, MN_TOU_*.
Compound names use descriptive uppercase snake case such as MN_SUDDEN_DEATH, MN_TRAINING_MODE, MN_PRIZE_INTERFACE, and MN_CAMERA_VS.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly rewrite or function matching tactic was present in the PR.
The change was intentionally binary-neutral: only a header enum was added, and the decomp.dev report stated GALE01 had no changes.
The enum's explicit order and offset comments provide a future matching aid for replacing raw constants in code without changing generated code.

review_feedback.feedback: Report for GALE01 showed 'No changes', confirming the header-only enum addition did not affect the built binary.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find raw minor scene ID constants in gm code that can be replaced with MinorSceneKind names.
Cross-reference MinorSceneKind ordering against the minor scene table to verify MN_0x6 and MN_0xA behavior.
Search for cutscene asset strings like visual0102Scene or Vi1202.dat to connect scene table entries to data files.
Look for uses of MinorScene, MinorSceneHandler, or class ID fields that should take MinorSceneKind instead of an integer type.
